title: Gyárfás–Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every forest $T$ and every integer $\kappa$, there exists $c$ such that $\chi(G) \leq c$ for every $T$-free graph $G$ with $\omega(G) \leq \kappa$.
context_text: Gyárfás [3] and Sumner [10] independently conjectured that for every tree $T$, the class of graphs not containing $T$ as an induced subgraph is $\chi$-bounded (chromatic numbers bounded above by a function of clique numbers). The conjecture remains open for general trees, though it has been proved for paths, subdivisions of stars, and certain other families.
paper_contribution: Proves that every $(1,\ldots,1,2,\ldots,2)$-multibroom satisfies the Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture, giving a common generalisation of the Kierstead–Penrice result (for $(1,\ldots,1)$-multibrooms) and the Kierstead–Zhu result (for $(2,\ldots,2)$-multibrooms).
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. XIII. New brooms
